mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS series reduces discount rates and eases financial conditions, so lower policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails if rate cuts coincide with worsening labor data and the equity response is negative.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average when FEDFUNDS is falling over the prior month, or if the signal's rolling Rank IC stays below 0.03 for any 24-month window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals labor-market deterioration and imminent earnings pressure, which predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest after a clear upward crossing rather than at stable high unemployment levels.
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE threshold crossings are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the conditional mean return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 standard deviation.

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in RSAFS and PAYEMS indicates broad consumer demand and payroll income growth, supporting near-term revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is not captured by either series alone; it requires both to improve together.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-PAYEMS improvement regime does not outperform single-series signals, or if positive 20-day SPX returns occur in fewer than 55% of matched instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in INDPRO after a prior contraction signals improving industrial capacity utilization and lower recession probability, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails when the rebound is driven only by inventory restocking without follow-through in demand.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 0.5% over 2 months after a decline
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO rebound episodes do not produce positive 60-day SPX returns on average, or if the hit rate is below 55% over at least 30 triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is elevated while FEDFUNDS remains below inflation, real policy rates are negative and financial conditions are too easy; that combination predicts lower S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days because it raises the odds of a policy repricing shock. The signal fails if inflation is high but policy is simultaneously tightening aggressively.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation runs above FEDFUNDS by > 2 pp
regime_hint: inflation overshoot with negative real rates
failure_condition: Reject if the high-inflation/low-policy-rate regime is followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the conditional mean return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: A broad slowdown in GDPC1 and RSAFS together marks weakening final demand and lower corporate sales expectations, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect is strongest when both series decelerate simultaneously rather than separately.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous deterioration in GDPC1 and RSAFS does not predict negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the joint signal underperforms a random baseline over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS series signals payroll losses that quickly feed into earnings revisions and risk-off behavior, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week. The effect should appear only after a clear monthly payroll contraction, not minor noise.
failure_condition: Reject if negative PAYEMS months are not followed by negative 5-day SPX returns more often than chance, or if the mean 5-day return in those months is nonnegative.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST and INDPRO both rise from depressed levels after a downturn, the economy is moving from contraction to early recovery and equity earnings expectations improve, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days. The mechanism fails if housing improves while industrial activity remains weak.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and INDPRO both rise after a prior 3-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if joint HOUST-INDPRO rebound episodes do not outperform single-variable signals, or if positive 60-day SPX returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered cases.
